statement.en: Promoting Simple Agents: Ensemble Methods for Event-Log Prediction
statement.summary: We compare lightweight automata-based models (n-grams) with neural architectures (LSTM, Transformer) for next-activity prediction in streaming event logs. Experiments on synthetic patterns and five real-world process mining datasets show that n-grams with appropriate context windows achieve comparable accuracy to neural models while requiring substantially fewer resources. Unlike windowed neural architectures, which show unstable performance patterns, n-grams provide stable and consistent accuracy. While we demonstrate that classical ensemble methods like voting improve n-gram performance, they require running many agents in parallel during inference, increasing memory consumption and latency. We propose an ensemble method, the promotion algorithm, that dynamically selects between two acti

proposer: Benedikt Bollig, Matthias Függer, Thomas Nowak et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper presents empirical findings (n-gram vs neural comparison) that are provisionally validated on specific datasets but generalizes across architectures and real-world processes—claims are testable and reproducible but not yet established as canonical in the field. The promotion algorithm is novel and under-specified in abstract (cut off), requiring peer review and broader validation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 軽い多数の代理人の加重平均が、重い単一の知恵に匹敵する不思議。
